title: ProjectMind: An Evidence-Frontier and Authority-Governed Project Continuity Architecture for AI Coding Agents
description: ProjectMind is a proposed local-first project-continuity and evidence-governance architecture for AI-assisted software development. For a target Git commit it computes a canonical Evidence Frontier, maintains claims with separate authenticity, confidence, authority, freshness, and completeness dimensions, records authority changes in an append-only ledger, and compiles task-specific knowledge into an immutable Change Consequence Contract. After a candidate is frozen, it independently reconstructs and reconciles the observed project-state and test delta and emits a Continuity Receipt. The proposal builds on established code graphs, temporal agent memory, impact analysis, MCP, provenance, policy, and attestation systems. Its contribution is the linked commit-DAG continuity-control chain, not the novelty of those components individually. This publication does not claim implementation, validation, patentability, legal compliance, peer review, certification, or production readiness.
notes: Independent Research Publication No. 4. v4.0 Public Research Edition. Not peer reviewed. Proposed architecture; implementation and benchmarks not supplied. Generative AI tools assisted research synthesis, prior-art comparison, consolidation, editing, consistency review, and publication preparation. The named author directed the work and is responsible for the released content and publication decision.
